title: Understanding the Node.js Event Loop
content: The Node.js event loop is the heart of Node's non-blocking architecture. It allows asynchronous operations while keeping the system efficient and scalable.
author: Owais

title: How HTTP Really Works Behind the Scenes
content: HTTP is a stateless protocol that works on request-response cycles. Understanding headers, status codes, and methods is critical for backend development.
author: Ahmed

title: Building a Server Without Express
content: Using the built-in http module, you can manually create a server, handle routes, parse URLs, and even build your own mini framework.
author: Sara

title: Understanding File System Module in Node.js
content: The fs module allows reading, writing, updating, and deleting files. It is commonly used for small projects or learning purposes.
author: Owais

title: Difference Between PUT and PATCH
content: PUT replaces the entire resource, whereas PATCH updates only specific fields. Choosing the right method improves API clarity.
author: Ali

title: Manual Body Parsing in Node.js
content: When handling POST requests without Express, you need to listen to the data and end events to collect and process the request body.
author: Fatima

title: What Happens When You Type a URL in Browser?
content: Typing a URL triggers DNS lookup, TCP handshake, TLS negotiation, and finally an HTTP request to the server.
author: Bilal

title: Understanding REST API Design Principles
content: REST APIs should use proper naming conventions, HTTP methods correctly, and meaningful status codes for better maintainability.
author: Owais
